name: AI Resume Screening - Email to Dashboard (v2 - hardened, free-tier)

nodes.name: Email Trigger (IMAP)
nodes.notes: Free: any mailbox works (Gmail free tier, Outlook free, or a self-hosted mail server). No paid service required. Point candidates to apply@yourdomain.com.

nodes.name: Log: Non-Resume Attachment
nodes.notes: FIX: previously this false branch had no connection at all — attachments that weren't pdf/doc/docx were silently dropped with zero trace. Now at least visible in execution history.

nodes.name: Extraction Valid?
nodes.notes: FIX: the critical gap again — the 'no valid resume file' error object from Extract Resume Files previously flowed straight into Extract Text from PDF with no binary to read, guaranteeing a crash. Now it's checked and short-circuited to a log node instead.

nodes.name: Route by File Type
nodes.notes: FIX: the original always ran 'extractFromPdf' regardless of actual file type, so every .doc/.docx submission would have failed extraction silently. Now routed by real extension.

nodes.name: Parse Valid?
nodes.notes: Same gate pattern once more: an empty/unreadable extraction now skips the paid AI call instead of sending blank text to the model.

nodes.name: Get Job Description
nodes.notes: FIX: this node was never wired to anything upstream in the original file (no incoming connection) — it was dead code that would never execute. Also removed the broken Merge node that used to sit between it and the AI node; this now runs as a direct, single-input step.

nodes.name: AI Resume Evaluation
nodes.notes: FIX: added the missing prompt entirely (this node had zero messages configured — it would have errored on every real execution). Added json_object mode, retry/backoff, and a fixed final_decision enum that now matches the dashboard's expected values exactly (SHORTLIST/REVIEW/REJECT). Cost note: swap model to 'gpt-4o-mini' for a materially cheaper run if screening volume is high and full gpt-4o accuracy isn't required.

nodes.name: Parse AI Response
nodes.notes: FIX: parses JSON directly first (reliable now under json_object mode), regex fallback kept only as a last resort. Now also catches an AI-node-level failure (after retries exhausted) and turns it into a clean error object.

parameters.jsCode:
const result = $input.first().json;

if (result.error) {
  return [{ json: { status: "error", error: result.error, details: result } }];
}

const validateScore = (s) => Math.min(100, Math.max(0, parseFloat(s) || 0));
const validateArray = (a) => Array.isArray(a) ? a : [];

const sanitized = {
  status: "ok",
  candidate_name: String(result.candidate_name || "Unknown"),
  email: String(result.email || "Not Found"),
  phone: String(result.phone || "Not Found"),
  job_title: String(result.job_title || "Not Specified"),
  overall_match: validateScore(result.overall_match),
  ats_score: validateScore(result.ats_score),
  technical_score: validateScore(result.technical_score),
  experience_score: validateScore(result.experience_score),
  education_score: validateScore(result.education_score),
  certification_score: validateScore(result.certification_score),
  project_score: validateScore(result.project_score),
  keyword_score: validateScore(result.keyword_score),
  soft_skill_score: validateScore(result.soft_skill_score),
  communication_score: validateScore(result.communication_score),
  leadership_score: validateScore(result.leadership_score),
  recommendation: String(result.recommendation || "Review Required"),
  risk_level: String(result.risk_level || "Medium"),
  interview_round: String(result.interview_round || "Technical Screening"),
  difficulty: String(result.difficulty || "Medium"),
  summary: String(result.summary || ""),
  matched_skills: validateArray(result.matched_skills),
  missing_skills: validateArray(result.missing_skills),
  nice_to_have_skills: validateArray(result.nice_to_have_skills),
  strengths: validateArray(result.strengths),
  weaknesses: validateArray(result.weaknesses),
  missing_certifications: validateArray(result.missing_certifications),
  resume_improvements: validateArray(result.resume_improvements),
  technical_questions: validateArray(result.technical_questions),
  hr_questions: validateArray(result.hr_questions),
  scenario_questions: validateArray(result.scenario_questions),
  behavioural_questions: validateArray(result.behavioural_questions),
  final_decision: String(result.final_decision || "REVIEW"),
  reason: String(result.reason || ""),
  file_name: result.file_name,
  email_from: result.email_from,
  email_subject: result.email_subject,
  email_date: result.email_date,
  _meta: result._meta || { evaluated_at: new Date().toISOString() }
};

return [{ json: sanitized }];

nodes.name: Sanitize Output
nodes.notes: Unchanged logic — already sound. default final_decision 'REVIEW' already matched the dashboard's expected enum, so no change needed here.

nodes.name: Build Dashboard Link
nodes.notes: FIX: replaced the 'Send to Dashboard' HTTP node, which posted to a fictional URL with an empty body ({} parameters — it was never actually filled in) and required hosting a backend to receive it. Host the dashboard HTML for free on GitHub Pages, Netlify, or Cloudflare Pages and put that URL here.

nodes.name: Route by Decision
nodes.notes: FIX: the original 3 rules were completely empty ({}) — the switch had no actual match conditions configured, so routing never worked as intended. Now matches final_decision against SHORTLIST / REVIEW / REJECT explicitly, with unmatched values falling back to the REVIEW branch (index 1) for human triage rather than being silently misrouted.

nodes.name: Store in Postgres
nodes.notes: FIX (replaces Google Sheets): calls the '08 - PostgreSQL Storage' sub-workflow you already have. Self-hosted Postgres is free (or free-tier hosted, e.g. Supabase/Neon) and gives you real querying/reporting instead of a flat spreadsheet with API quotas.

parameters.subject: ✅ Candidate SHORTLISTED: {{ $json.candidate_name }} - {{ $json.job_title }}

nodes.name: Email: Shortlist HR
nodes.notes: FIX: added the missing to/from/body fields and a link to the dashboard. SMTP is free with Gmail (App Password), Outlook, or your existing mail provider — no paid email service needed.

parameters.subject: ⚠️ Candidate for REVIEW: {{ $json.candidate_name }} - {{ $json.job_title }}
❌ Candidate REJECTED: {{ $json.candidate_name }} - {{ $json.job_title }}
